prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. Parliamentary elections were held in Colombia on 9 March 2014 to elect members to both chambers of Congress. The nationwide constituency for the 102-member Senate was contested, as well as the 166 seats of the House of Representatives, plus the delegates to the Andean Parliament. There were 773 candidates for the Senate, 1,528 candidates for the House of Representatives, and 23 candidates for the five Colombian seats in the Andean Parliament. 32,795,962 Colombians had been registered to vote in the elections by the cut-off date of 25 January 2014.The elections were notable for the decision by former president Álvaro Uribe to stand for the Senate, the first ex-president in modern Colombian history to run for Congress afterwards. Uribe is constitutionally barred from standing for president again, having already
B. On March 9, 2014, Colombia conducted parliamentary elections to select representatives for both its legislative bodies, Congress. The electoral process involved competition for the 102 positions in the Senate, which constituted a single national constituency. Additionally, all 166 seats in the House of Representatives were up for election, alongside the delegates who would represent the nation in the Andean Parliament. A total of 773 individuals vied for a Senate seat, while 1,528 candidates sought election to the House of Representatives. Furthermore, 23 candidates competed for the five designated Colombian seats within the Andean Parliament. By the voter registration deadline of January 25, 2014, a substantial 32,795,962 Colombian citizens were officially registered and eligible to cast their votes in these elections. A particularly noteworthy aspect of these elections was the decision of former president Álvaro Uribe to seek a position in the Senate. This marked a significant historical event, as he became the first ex-president in Colombia's contemporary history to pursue election to Congress subsequent to his presidential term. Uribe's ability to run for the presidency again is constitutionally prohibited, given his prior incumbency.
C. On 9 March 2014, Colombian lawmakers elected members to both chambers of Congress through Parliamentary elections. The electoral process included the contested nationwide constituency of the 102-member Senate, 166 seats of the House of Representatives, plus delegates to the Andean Parliament. The election saw 773 candidates for the Senate, 1,528 candidates for the House of Representatives, and 23 candidates for the five Colombian seats in the Andean Parliament. By the end of the election, 32,795,962 people in the country had registered to vote.

Answer:
